extraction_note: Q&A section is largely in Italian ([Foreign Language]). English content limited to prepared remarks by CEO Pietro Giuliani and CFO Marco Malcontenti, plus a few English Q&A exchanges.

claims.claim_text: Azimut has the ability to reprice its products without losing customers, demonstrated by successfully repricing 80% of AUM from 1.2-1.8% to 1.5-2.5% in 2005 when Italian regulations changed, retaining all customers.
claims.speaker: Pietro Giuliani
claims.evidence_quote: In 2005... we repriced our products... from 1.20%, 1.80%, the price of Italian products at the time, to 1.50%, 2.50%... on 80% of our customer. We repriced. We have kept all our customers... We get the customer. We get the distribution system. So we can reprice without troubles.

claims.claim_text: Azimut's proprietary financial adviser network provides structural distribution advantage: ranked #1 in Italy by net inflows per financial adviser (EUR 1 million per adviser), and the network's loyalty is structurally superior to bank employees during market crises.
claims.speaker: Pietro Giuliani
claims.evidence_quote: We rank #1 in Italy in term of productivity. I mean, net inflows per financial advisers are EUR 1 million... when you have to face a crisis, I think that the behavior of a network of financial adviser prior bankruptcies strongly different from the reaction of a normal bank employee.

claims.claim_text: Azimut's investment performance has consistently exceeded industry average by approximately 1% per annum over 20+ years (since 1992), even through Internet crisis and 2007-08 financial crisis, and currently outperforms by 200bps year-to-date.
claims.speaker: Pietro Giuliani
claims.evidence_quote: From beginning on 2013, we are performing more than 1% above industry per annum... if you want, give a look to longer than 20 years, let's start from the beginning... we again overperformed of about 1% above industry per annum, including Internet crisis, 2007, '08... 200 basis point after fees on top of industry average year-to-date.

claims.claim_text: Azimut's performance fee structure (variable ~50-60bps vs. lower fixed management fees) is a differentiated pricing model that aligns manager and client interests, as evidenced by CEO personally investing 100% of EUR 40 million personal proceeds into Azimut Luxembourg funds at full performance fees.
claims.speaker: Pietro Giuliani
claims.evidence_quote: As CEO of this company... I have invested 100% of the EUR 40 million I cashed, net of taxes... into Azimut Luxembourg funds without any discount on performance fees... it's not an idea or a theory. It's the result of 25 years in Italian markets by this pricing.

claims.claim_text: Azimut maintains a loyal and educated client base that values net performance over cost, enabling the company to maintain a differentiated performance-fee pricing structure for 25 years without customer attrition.
claims.speaker: Pietro Giuliani
claims.evidence_quote: It's 25 years that we have this kind of price in Italy, is appreciated by our customers. They are educated not to look at cost, but to look at net performance after cost.

claims.claim_text: Azimut doubled AUM in 3.5 years (to EUR 35.2 billion by H1 2015) driven by net inflows of EUR 4 billion in H1 2015, with international operations contributing EUR 1.6 billion, demonstrating scalability of the distribution model.
claims.speaker: Pietro Giuliani
claims.evidence_quote: We have assets under management, EUR 35.2 billion. Total assets increased by 17% in the first half of 2015 thanks to net inflows, about EUR 4 billion in 6 months, helped by EUR 1.6 billion of abroad operations... We have doubled total asset in 3.5 years.

claims.claim_text: Azimut's new product launch capability demonstrated by new fund reaching EUR 600 million in AUM within one month of launch.
claims.speaker: Pietro Giuliani
claims.evidence_quote: The last product we have launched, in 1 month, it reached more than EUR 600 million in terms of asset under management.
